c74object.name: tap.5comb~
c74object.digest: Five parallel comb filters with master controls and preset morphing
c74object.description: The tap.5comb~ object is a bank of five parallel feedback comb filters modeled on the GRM Tools Classic "Comb Filters" plugin. Each comb has a resonant frequency, a resonance (ring time), and a lowpass filter inside its feedback loop; master multipliers scale the frequencies, resonances, and lowpass cutoffs of all five at once. Every parameter is interpolated per sample, so slider sweeps and preset recalls glide continuously with no zipper noise — sweep the master frequency and the resonances slide like a Doppler shift. Sixteen preset slots can be stored and morphed between over a settable interpolation time. The warp and phase attributes add the modern GRM Comb's character controls: warp stretches the resonance partials from purely harmonic toward inharmonic/metallic (an allpass inside each feedback loop; the fundamental stays in tune), and phase moves the pickup point in the loop so that at 100 only the odd harmonics ring. The delays are fractional (Hermite interpolated), resonance maps to ring time on a logarithmic curve so the control is musical across its whole range, and the feedback path is kept clean (no clipper; feedback capped just below unity with DC control), so full resonance rings effectively forever without distortion. Note: at extreme warp combined with very high tunings the loop cannot get shorter than the allpass dispersion and the pitch flattens.

method.name: store
method.description: Store the current parameters in a preset slot (1-16).

method.name: recall
method.description: Morph all parameters from their current values to a stored preset (1-16). The optional second argument overrides the morph time in milliseconds; without it the interp attribute is used. Recalling a new preset mid-morph re-targets smoothly, and moving an individual parameter during a morph overrides the morph for that parameter only.

method.name: clear
method.description: Clear the delay buffers and filter state of all five combs.

attribute.name: freq1
attribute.description: Frequency of comb 1 in Hz (45-20000). Likewise freq2-freq5 for the other combs.

attribute.name: freq2
attribute.description: Frequency of comb 2 in Hz (45-20000).

attribute.name: freq3
attribute.description: Frequency of comb 3 in Hz (45-20000).

attribute.name: freq4
attribute.description: Frequency of comb 4 in Hz (45-20000).

attribute.name: freq5
attribute.description: Frequency of comb 5 in Hz (45-20000).

attribute.name: res1
attribute.description: Resonance of comb 1 (0-100). 0 is no resonance; 100 rings effectively forever. Maps to ring time on a logarithmic curve, so the control is musical across its whole travel and a comb keeps its ring time as its frequency sweeps. Likewise res2-res5.

attribute.name: res2
attribute.description: Resonance of comb 2 (0-100).

attribute.name: res3
attribute.description: Resonance of comb 3 (0-100).

attribute.name: res4
attribute.description: Resonance of comb 4 (0-100).

attribute.name: res5
attribute.description: Resonance of comb 5 (0-100).

attribute.name: lp1
attribute.description: Cutoff frequency in Hz (0-20000) of the lowpass filter in comb 1's feedback loop. High values keep the resonance bright and metallic; low values smooth and darken it. Likewise lp2-lp5.

attribute.name: lp2
attribute.description: Cutoff (Hz) of the lowpass in comb 2's feedback loop (0-20000).

attribute.name: lp3
attribute.description: Cutoff (Hz) of the lowpass in comb 3's feedback loop (0-20000).

attribute.name: lp4
attribute.description: Cutoff (Hz) of the lowpass in comb 4's feedback loop (0-20000).

attribute.name: lp5
attribute.description: Cutoff (Hz) of the lowpass in comb 5's feedback loop (0-20000).

attribute.name: freq
attribute.description: Master frequency multiplier (0-2) applied to freq1-freq5. 2 transposes all five combs an octave up; 0 pins them at the 5 Hz floor.

attribute.name: res
attribute.description: Master resonance multiplier (0-2) applied to res1-res5.

attribute.name: lp
attribute.description: Master lowpass multiplier (0-2) applied to lp1-lp5.

attribute.name: mix
attribute.description: Dry/wet crossfade (0 = dry, 100 = wet), equal-power.

attribute.name: gain
attribute.description: Output gain in dB.

attribute.name: warp
attribute.description: Partial warp (0-100): an allpass inside each feedback loop stretches the upper resonance partials from purely harmonic (0) toward inharmonic and metallic (100), like a stiffening string. The fundamental tuning is compensated. 0 matches the GRM Classic behavior.

attribute.name: phase
attribute.description: Pickup position (0-100): moves where the output is tapped from the delay loop. At 100, phase cancellations leave only the odd harmonics ringing. 0 matches the GRM Classic behavior.

attribute.name: interp
attribute.description: Default preset-morph time in milliseconds used by the recall message.

attribute.name: smooth
attribute.description: Anti-zipper interpolation time in milliseconds applied to direct parameter changes.

attribute.name: bypass
attribute.description: Pass the input through unprocessed.

attribute.name: mute
attribute.description: Silence the output.
